DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT RICHARD SZETO's SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. Defendant has not met his burden of showing that the causes of action asserted against him arise from protected activity. Defendant's contention that his "involvement" in or status as a party to an action filed against him constitutes protected speech on a public matter is supported neither by coherent argument nor applicable authority. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR # 8098

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT RICHARD SZETO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. The Complaint fails to allege any conduct by Defendant to support any of the causes of action asserted against him. The Complaint also fails to allege any facts to support the formation or existence of a conspiracy between Defendant and any other named Defendant. Further, Plaintiff bases Defendant's liability as a coconspirator on an erroneous legal basis-i.e., that Defendant became a member of the alleged conspiracy by and through his involvement in or status as a party to an action filed against him. Thus, the Complaint cannot be cured by amendment because such conduct could not form nor further any such alleged conspiracy. Moreover, with respect to any claims resulting from alleged violations of the Ralph Act (Civ. Code 51.7) or the Bane Act (Civ. Code 52.1), Plaintiff fails to allege that any violent acts or threatened violent acts were committed by anyone, let alone the moving party. Plaintiff alleges verbal abuse by another name defendant but this is not sufficient to state a constitutional deprivation of rights per Title 42 U.S.C. 1983. (Oltarzewski v. Ruggiero (9th Cir. 1987) 830 F.2d 136, 139. Nor are any facts alleged linking the moving party to this conduct other than the bare conclusion that he is a coconspirator. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR # 8098 =302/ehg
